sections.paragraph:
Σολομών, ῶνος, ὁ (so predom. in NT and Joseph. [even in quotations of Joseph. fr. Dios, pre-Christian: 785 Fgm. 1, 114 Jac. =C. Ap. 1, 114f] and Menander of Ephesus, III b.c.: 783 Fgm. 1, 120 Jac. [C. Ap. 1, 120]; Eupolemus the Jew [II b.c.]: 723 Fgm. 2b 8ff Jac. [in Eus., PE 9, 30, 8ff]; Just., Tat., Mel.; Christian magical pap PGM II, P 17, 10 p. 218 [Henrichs], rare in the LXX) and Σολομῶν, ῶντος, ὁ ([for accent cp. Ξενοφῶν] Cass. Dio. 69, 14, 2 τὸ μνημεῖον τοῦ Σολομῶντος; Zosimus: Hermet. IV p. 111, 13; Ac 3:11 and 5:12 as στοὰ Σολομῶντος); only as v.l. the indecl. Σολομών Mt 1:6 and Σαλωμών Ac 7:47 (the latter is the normal form in the LXX) or Σαλομών Sin.; s. Tdf., Proleg. 104; 110: W-H., App. 158; B-D-F §53, 1; 55, 2; W-S. §10, 4; Mlt-H. 146f; Thackeray p. 165 f (שְׁלֹמֹה) Solomon, son of Bathsheba and David, and the latter’s successor, known for his love of splendor Mt 6:29; Lk 12:27 and for his wisdom Mt 12:42ab; Lk 11:31ab. Builder of the first temple in Jerusalem Ac 7:47. There was also a colonnade named for him in Herod’s temple J 10:23; Ac 3:11; 5:12 (cp. Jos., Ant. 20, 221, Bell. 5, 185). In the genealogy of Jesus Mt 1:6f. Pauly-W. VIII Suppl. 660–704; BHHW III 1651–53.—GKnoppers, The Vanishing Solomon: JBL 116, ’97, 19–44 (anc. Israel). M-M. TW.

predom. predom. = predominant(ly)

NT NT = New Testament

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

  1. fr. = from

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Zosimus Zosimus = Z. alchem, III/IV a.d.—List 5

Hermet. Hermet. , s. Herm. Wr.

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

W-H. W-H. = BWestcott/FHort—List 1, beg.

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

Thackeray Thackeray = HT., A Grammar of the OT in Greek according to the Septuagint—List 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

Suppl.

Suppl. = Supplement (to serials)

suppl. = supplement, supplied (in grammatical references)

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

JBL JBL = Journal of Biblical Literature—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 934
